questions.question: Explain the difference between stack overflow and stack underflow with examples.
keyPoints: Stack overflow: trying to push into a full stack
Stack underflow: trying to pop from an empty stack
Example: In array of size 5, overflow when top==4, underflow when top==-1

questions.question: Convert the infix expression (A + B) * C to postfix notation showing the steps.
Describe the operations performed in a circular queue when an element is deleted.
keyPoints: Check if queue is empty (front > rear)
Increment front using formula: front = (front + 1) % MAXSIZE
If front becomes equal to rear after deletion, reset queue

questions.question: Explain why circular queue is preferred over linear queue with an example.
keyPoints: Linear queue wastes space after dequeue operations
Example: Queue of size 5, after adding 5 elements (front=0,rear=4), then removing 3 (front=3)
Cannot add new elements even though 3 positions are empty
Circular queue wraps around and uses all spaces efficiently

questions.question: Write an algorithm to check if a given string with parentheses is balanced using stack.
keyPoints: For each character: if opening bracket, push to stack
If closing bracket, check if stack top matches, then pop
At end, if stack is empty, parentheses are balanced

questions.question: Convert the prefix expression + - * 2 3 4 5 to its value showing each step.
Write a C program to implement a stack using array with operations: push, pop, display, and check for overflow/underflow.
keyPoints: Implement push(): check overflow, increment top, add element
Implement pop(): check underflow, remove element, decrement top
Implement display(): traverse from top to bottom

questions.question: Explain the algorithm to convert infix expression to postfix expression with an example A + B * C - D / E.
keyPoints: Read infix expression from left to right
If operator: pop operators with higher/equal precedence, then push current
At end: pop all remaining operators

questions.question: Implement a circular queue with enqueue and dequeue operations. Explain how it overcomes the limitations of linear queue.
keyPoints: Define structure with front, rear, array[MAXSIZE]
Enqueue: check if queue full using (rear+1)%MAXSIZE == front
Dequeue: check if queue empty using front == -1
Overcomes: Reuses empty spaces by wrapping around using modular arithmetic

questions.question: Evaluate the postfix expression: 10 5 * 3 2 + - showing step-by-step evaluation using stack.
